key_points: Work is done when a force is applied on a body and it is displaced in the direction of the force.
Work is zero if there is no displacement or if force is perpendicular to displacement.
Energy is the capacity to do work.
Kinetic energy (KE) is energy of motion: KE = 1/2 mv².
Potential energy (PE) is energy due to position: PE = mgh.
Law of conservation of energy: Energy can neither be created nor destroyed, only transformed.
Power is the rate of doing work: P = W/t.
Machines help us do work more efficiently; efficiency = (useful work output / total work input) × 100%.
formulas: Efficiency: Efficiency (%) = (Useful work output / Total work input) × 100%
Elastic potential energy: PE_spring = 1/2 k x²
examples: A force of 10 N moves a box 5 m along the force direction: W = 10 × 5 = 50 J
A 2 kg body moving at 3 m/s: KE = 1/2 × 2 × 3² = 9 J
A body of mass 5 kg lifted 10 m: PE = 5 × 9.8 × 10 = 490 J
A person does 200 J work in 20 s: Power = 200/20 = 10 W
Efficiency of machine: Input energy = 600 J, Output = 500 J → Efficiency = (500/600)×100 ≈ 83.33%
exam_tips: Check if displacement is along the force to decide if work is done.
Always convert units correctly (kg, m, s) before calculation.
For KE and PE, identify whether the body is moving or at height.
Use energy conservation for problems with motion on slopes or pendulums.
